body.purpose: Running text and form values (16px, weight 400).

label.purpose: Form-field labels; the quietest voice (1em, weight 200).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The default action button: white text on Action Blue, slightly rounded, flat.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { color: #fff; background: #3f88c5; border: 0; border-radius: 0.25em; padding: 0.5em 1em; margin: 0.25em; font: inherit; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #3470a2; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3f88c5; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: #3f88c599; cursor: default; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive action: white text on Danger Red. Same shape as primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { color: #fff; background: #d00000; border: 0; border-radius: 0.25em; padding: 0.5em 1em; margin: 0.25em; font: inherit; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-danger:active { background: #ab0000; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d00000; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-danger:disabled { background: #d0000099; cursor: default; }

components.name: Link Button
components.description: Text-only action in Action Blue; underline on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-link { color: #3f88c5; background: transparent; border: 0; padding: 0; font: inherit; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-link:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-btn-link:active { color: #3470a2; } .ds-btn-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3f88c5; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Borderless field with a single hairline underline that turns blue on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { border: 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 0.125em 0.25em; width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; font: inherit; background: #fff; color: #000; } .ds-input:focus { border-bottom-color: #3f88c5; outline: none; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #999; }

components.name: Textarea
components.description: Full hairline border, auto-growing, no resize handle; blue border on focus.
components.css: .ds-textarea { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 0.125em 0.25em; width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; resize: none; font: inherit; background: #fff; color: #000; } .ds-textarea:focus { border-color: #3f88c5; outline: none; }

components.name: Alert (Danger)
components.description: Bordered notice; 2px border and text share the state hue (red for danger, amber for highlight).
components.html: That invitation link has expired.
components.css: .ds-alert-danger { border: 2px solid #d00000; color: #d00000; border-radius: 0.25em; padding: 0.5em 1em; margin: 0.5em 0; font: inherit; background: #fff; }

components.name: Progress Bar
components.description: Hairline-grey track; fill is green at 100%, amber below.
components.css: .ds-progress { background: #ddd; height: 8px; width: 100%; margin: 6px 0; } .ds-progress__bar { background: #ffba08; height: 100%; } .ds-progress__bar[style*="100%"] { background: #39b54a; }

components.name: List Item
components.description: Hairline-separated row with an optional grey hover wash.
components.css: .ds-list-item { border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 0.5em 1em; font: inherit; color: #000; background: #fff; } .ds-list-item:hover { background: #efefef; }

components.name: Header Bar
components.description: The app's one persistent chrome: navy band, white title, logo at 4em.
components.html: Lessons from Luke
components.css: .ds-header { display: flex; align-items: center; background: #1c3144; color: #fff; padding: 1em; } .ds-header__logo { display: inline-block; width: 4em; height: 4em; margin-right: 0.8em; border-radius: 0.25em; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); } .ds-header__title { margin: 0; font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: 700; }

narrative.overview: This is a working tool, not a showpiece. A field manual earns trust by being legible, predictable, and rugged — it works on a folding table in a village with spotty power as readily as on an office desk. Every screen exists to serve one job: get translated curriculum out of a translator's head and into an exportable lesson, in their own writing system, without the interface getting in the way. The system is deliberately plain: flat (no shadows, no gradients), monochrome-typed (a single Helvetica family), built from a small consistent kit of styled-components primitives. State is communicated through a tight, named color set: blue for action, green for done, amber for in-progress/attention, red for danger. The current directive is to maintain and extend this established style, not to redesign it.
keyCharacteristics: Flat by default — no shadows, no gradients; depth from borders and the dark header band alone.
One type family — Helvetica at a small set of sizes; hierarchy through size and weight, never new faces.
Information-carrying color — the Colors.ts set is functional (action / done / attention / danger), used sparingly on white.
Em-based, fluid spacing — sizing scales with font-size; comfortable, not dense.
Script-agnostic — must host any writing system and reading direction without breaking.

rules.name: The Color-Means-Something Rule
rules.body: No color is used decoratively. If a hue appears, it is reporting state — blue (actionable), green (done), amber (in progress / attention), red (danger).

rules.name: The Single-Action-Color Rule
rules.body: Blue is the only interactive color. Don't introduce a second accent; if everything is highlighted, nothing is.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy comes from size and weight within Helvetica — never from adding a second typeface. There is no display font and no mono font.

rules.name: The Script-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Never hard-code Latin-only assumptions into line-height, truncation, or font stacks. The editor must render right-to-left and complex scripts cleanly.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat and separated by hairlines, not shadows. If a new component reaches for a drop shadow to stand out, the answer is a border or whitespace instead.

dos: Do build new screens from the base-components/ kit and the Colors.ts tokens. The in-progress invitation workflow is not a style reference; it is unfinished and should be brought up to match this system.
Do keep surfaces flat and separate them with 1px #ddd hairlines or whitespace.
Do use color only to report state: blue = action, green = done, amber = attention, red = danger.
Do keep body text at Ink (#000) on white. Reserve Mute Grey (#999) for placeholders and disabled text only.

Do restore a visible keyboard focus indicator on buttons and inputs (the kit currently sets outline: none). WCAG 2.1 AA is the target; add a focus ring in the system's blue without otherwise altering the look.
Do pair every state color with text, an icon, or a shape so meaning survives for color-blind users.
Do keep the UI font neutral and let any writing system or reading direction (incl. RTL) render cleanly inside it.
donts: Don't add a second accent color or use any hue decoratively.
Don't introduce shadows, gradients, glassmorphism, or hype animation — the flashy-trendy-startup look this product rejects.
Don't reach for childish church clip-art, crowded corporate-SaaS dashboards, or ornate religious skeuomorphism (gilt, parchment, stained glass, faux leather).
Don't add a second typeface or a display/mono font; hierarchy is size + weight within Helvetica.
Don't lighten body text toward gray for elegance — it breaks AA contrast and legibility.
Don't assume Latin text, left-to-right layout, modern hardware, or fast bandwidth.
